text: The patient is positioned in a forty-five degree left up orientation for a laparoscopic, spleen-preserving distal pancreatectomy. The video shows the placement of laparoscopic ports on the patient's abdomen.
The surgeon enters the lesser sac and exposes the pancreas. The lower border of the pancreas is divided and mobilized from the retroperitoneum, cirrhotas fascia, and the mesocolon.
Care must be taken to avoid injury to the pancreas and repeated sharp and blunt dissection helps in achieving this objective.
The surgeon carefully dissects the upper part of the pancreas away from the splenic vessels, ensuring no injury occurs to these vessels during the procedure.
The surgeon continues to mobilize the pancreas, revealing the tumor. Gentle layer-by-layer mobilization is performed to avoid injury to the pancreas.

The surgeon divides the remaining attachments at the upper border, fully mobilizing the distal pancreas. An endo-GIA stapler, size 60 with white cartridges, is carefully positioned and fired slowly with graduated compression over two to three minutes to ensure hemostasis and minimize the risk of ductal leak.
The surgical specimen is removed, and gel foam is applied to the suture line. A drain is placed near the pancreatic remnant.
